{"id":"project-LICHEN-45iz","title":"to_dict leaks mutable reference to errors list","description":"## Location
python/src/lichen/sim/metrics.py:85

## Description
The to_dict() method returns a direct reference to self.errors rather than a copy. Unlike the set fields (packet_hashes_sent, packet_hashes_received, unique_peers) which are converted to new sorted lists, the errors list is included directly. Any caller that modifies the returned dictionary's errors key will corrupt the internal NodeMetrics state.

## Failure Scenario
Caller receives metrics dict via `d = node.metrics.to_dict()`, then calls `d['errors'].clear()` or `d['errors'].append('extra')`. The internal self.errors list is modified, corrupting the metrics for all future reads and serializations.

## Category

{"id":"project-LICHEN-45iz","title":"to_dict leaks mutable reference to errors list","description":"## Location
python/src/lichen/sim/metrics.py:85

## Description
The to_dict() method returns a direct reference to self.errors rather than a copy. Unlike the set fields (packet_hashes_sent, packet_hashes_received, unique_peers) which are converted to new sorted lists, the errors list is included directly. Any caller that modifies the returned dictionary's errors key will corrupt the internal NodeMetrics state.

## Failure Scenario
Caller receives metrics dict via `d = node.metrics.to_dict()`, then calls `d['errors'].clear()` or `d['errors'].append('extra')`. The internal self.errors list is modified, corrupting the metrics for all future reads and serializations.

new_value: Fixed by using sorted(self.errors) for consistency and to avoid reference sharing
